methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Alpine Eagle, a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Jan-Hendrik Boelens and Timo Breuer, has raised €10.25M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of 10 private and 2 public comparables operating in the same sector and stage band, including peers such as Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

The peer-median method, applied across four same-sector and same/adjacent-stage private comparables, yields a median valuation of $225M. The peer sample spans Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), Ares Industries ($150M), and Tenna Systems ($60M), establishing a range of $60M to $500M. This single triangulation method forms the basis for the current analysis given data availability constraints.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $191M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $225M. Key uncertainties include the limited methodological depth—only peer-median comparable is fully applicable—and stage-mixing within the peer set, where three comparables are at Series A versus Alpine Eagle's Seed position. Additional clarity on revenue, user metrics, or product maturity would refine the range. The $225M median implies a post-money valuation approximately 22x the amount raised, which warrants validation against Seed-stage norms in Defense & Security specifically.
